postData.contentHtml:
The 2028 presidential race might seem a distant blip on the political radar, but early predictions and polling are already setting the stage for what promises to be a fascinating contest. Recent discussions have been ignited by Senator JD Vance, who has publicly stated his belief that Representative Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez (AOC) will emerge as the leading Democratic candidate in 2028, rather than California Governor Gavin Newsom.

Adding fuel to the speculative fire, AOC herself has hinted at a potential run, even expressing a desire to face Vance on the national stage. This exchange underscores the high-profile nature of these figures, even in the nascent stages of the election cycle.

New Hampshire Poll Reveals Shifting Sands

A recent poll conducted in New Hampshire (June 24-25, 2026) by the Saint Anselm College Survey Center offers a glimpse into these early dynamics:

  • Democratic Primary: Former Transportation Secretary Pete Buttigieg currently holds the lead with 29%. However, AOC has shown a significant surge, rising to second place with 17% (up from 10% in March), now surpassing Gavin Newsom, who sits at 11% (down from 15% in March).
  • Republican Primary: On the Republican side, Vice President JD Vance leads the field with 37%, followed by Secretary of State Marco Rubio at 30%. Notably, Vance's lead has narrowed from 19 points in March to a tighter 7 points.

Broader Political Climate

The poll also provided insights into the general political landscape:

  • President Donald Trump's approval and favorability ratings have seen a notable decline, dropping to 38% and 37% respectively, a departure from his historical stability on these metrics.
  • Democrats currently hold a lead on the generic ballot (49% to 39% Republican), with this gap widening by two points since the last survey.
  • Voters express greater trust in Democrats regarding affordability issues, and Democratic voters demonstrate higher enthusiasm for the upcoming elections.
  • Despite the broader challenges for Republicans, New Hampshire's Republican Governor Kelly Ayotte remains a strong contender, maintaining a positive approval rating (49%) and leading her Democratic challenger (45% to 37%) for re-election.

While no candidates have officially declared their intentions for 2028, these early indicators suggest a dynamic political environment, with new faces and evolving voter sentiments already shaping the narrative for the next presidential election.
postData.title: 2028 Watch: Vance, AOC, and Newsom in Early Presidential Scramble
postData.summary: Early polling for the 2028 presidential election is stirring the pot, with JD Vance predicting an AOC-led Democratic field and a new New Hampshire poll showing shifting dynamics for both parties. While it's still years away, the battle lines for the next White House race are already being drawn.

sources.title: JD Vance's chances of beating AOC and Newsom in new 2028 poll
